old_claim.note: Scholasticism is contextual infrastructure or shared knowledge, not a strict hard prerequisite.

new_claim.note: Scholastic dialectical methods supplied a shared commentary style for harmonizing authorities; this is methodological context rather than a strict prerequisite.

source_shape.source_locator: Opening section says glossators approached legal sources dialectically and identifies that method as characteristic of medieval scholasticism.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source connects glossator legal interpretation to dialectical harmonization of authorities and explicitly labels that approach characteristic of medieval scholasticism.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This supports scholasticism as a shared method context for the glossator edge, but remains partial because the citation is a broad overview rather than a specialized legal-history monograph.

invariant_preserved_or_changed: Preserved: legal_commentary_glossators keeps scholasticism as common methodological context, not as a required institutional or technical component.
would_reject_if: The node were narrowed to only pre-scholastic Roman legal commentary.
A dedicated dialectical_legal_method node were added and should replace the broader scholasticism prerequisite.
A stronger legal-history source showed the glossator method should not be characterized as scholastic.
short_reason: Glossator harmonization uses a scholastic dialectical method.
